Question Text: Is there a formalized risk governance policy approved by management that defines the Enterprise Risk Management program requirements?
Does the risk governance program include risk management policies, procedures, and internal controls?
Does the risk governance program include range of assets to include: people, processes, data, and technology?
Does the governing body define the accountabilities of management and obligations of the Board of Directors for risk management?
Is the risk governance program approved by senior management and/or board of directors?
Is training provided to employees regarding risk expectations and their obligations?
Does the organization's risk management program include processes that analyze risk at the enterprise level keeping stakeholders and senior management informed of risk management decisions?
Are there Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) and/or groups assigned to assess risk for distinct categories (e.g., operational, reputational, regulatory, technology, privacy, financial, etc.)?

Is there a formalized Risk Assessment process that requires identifying, documenting, quantifying, categorizing, and prioritizing cybersecurity risks based on risk acceptance levels relevant to the organization that has been communicated?
Does the risk assessment process identify and monitor inherent and residual risk?
Are risks to critical processes and entities reassessed annually?
Does the organization have a process in place for documenting and approving the rationale and/or exclusion of specific controls in the risk management program?
Is there a formal process in the risk management program to identify, accept, prioritize, manage, and assign ownership with approved action plans for risk decisions?

Does the Enterprise Risk Management program include measures for defining, monitoring, and reporting risk metrics to the appropriate stakeholders (e.g., KRIs, KPIs, SLAs, performance benchmarks, maturity levels, etc.)?
Is there a third party risk management program that is reviewed and approved by management which includes 4th and Nth parties as part of the program?
Are there established third-party risk management standards or procedures for selecting, overseeing, and assessing fourth/nth parties within the scope of provided services?
Have policies, standards, and procedures for implementing the third party risk management program been reviewed and approved by senior management?
For all organizational entities (e.g., vendor's vendors, subcontractors, fourth parties, Nth parties) is there a contractual relationship that extends obligations to each entity?
Does the third-party risk management program require Confidentiality and/or Non-Disclosure Agreements from all outsourced relationships(e.g. fourth/Nth party)?
Do third-party contracts include all applicable privacy and security control requirements including those with fourth/Nth party?
Do third-party party contracts include incident and data breach notifications including those with fourth/Nth party?
Does the third party risk management program include creating and maintaining a third party inventory based on information and/or risk classification?
Does the third party risk management program have a governing body accountable for the selection, oversight and risk assessment of vendors or service providers?
Are the activities of third party personnel monitored for potential security incidents?
Does the third party risk management program include definition of required contract development, adherence and management policies and processes?
Does the third party risk management program include the definition of a third party risk assessment process?
Does the third party risk management program include definition and a process for ongoing monitoring and review of third party risk?
Are third-parties or service providers evaluated for reassessment when there are material changes to risk posture, service offerings or contracts?
Does the third party risk management program include assessments performed on all potential entities before entering into contracts with them?
Does the third party risk management program include requirements to review a vendor's third party risk program?
Does the third party risk management program require notification for any new or changed outsourced contract relationships?
Does the third party risk program require notification by each party of any changes affecting services rendered?
Does the third party risk management program require background checks for service provider contractors and subcontractors?
Does the third party risk management program include an assigned individual or group responsible for capturing, maintaining, and tracking Information Security, Privacy, or other issues?
Is there a documented, approved cybersecurity risk management policy communicated across the organization?
Has the organization determined and documented the scope of the information security program considering relevant internal and external issues, and business activities?
Does the information security policy have clearly defined and measurable objectives that are monitored, updated and recorded (as necessary), and communicated with all constituents?
Are information security policies, standards, and procedures based on accepted controls, frameworks, and industry practices?
Does the organization have a documents and records management program with an assigned owner required by the information security program to ensure it is adequately controlled and protected?
Does the information security program establish requirements for the protection of information that is accessed, processed, stored, or transmitted on external systems?

Has senior management designated and communicated a qualified owner (e.g., Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)) of the information security program who ensures enforcement of the policy, and reports on the program's performance to senior management?
Does the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) or designated owner(s) report annually to the board of directors or other senior officer, on the cybersecurity program and material risks?
Is there a management-approved information security process for handling deviations, exceptions and exemptions to information security policies, standards and procedures?
Does the information security program define and set objectives and principles to address the unauthorized disclosure, misuse, alteration, destruction, or other compromise of client-scoped data?

Does senior management clearly identify and communicate the roles and responsibilities for information security policies, processes and procedures to assigned personnel before being granted access to the organization's information and assets?
Have any of the Information Security and IT processes been outsourced?
Do the information security policies and procedures include requirements for third-party risk management?
Is there a documented risk assessment process for information security with consistent and comparable results?
Does the information security assessment process include residual risk acceptance responsibilities?
Does the information security risk assessment process address the risks to architecture, infrastructure, software, operations, concentration, business model, cybersecurity, and IT services strategy?

Does assessing infosec risks involve identifying owners, analyzing risks, considering potential loss, determining consequences and likelihood of reoccurrence, comparing results to criteria, and prioritizing treatment?
Do all projects involving systems, applications, and platforms go through an information security assessment?
Are information security personnel responsible for the design of information technology systems, processes, and architecture required to meet information security requirements?
Are information security personnel responsible for the review of information security policies and standards on a scheduled basis (e.g., annually)?
Do information security personnel review the effectiveness of information security policy implementation and manage instances of non-compliance with security policies across the entire organization?
Does senior management ensure all personnel are provided with a confidential channel for reporting violations of the information security policy?

Does the organization's senior management demonstrate leadership and commitment concerning the information security program by ensuring the policy objectives and requirements align with the strategic direction of the entire organization, and are integrated into the organization's processes?
Does the organization's board of directors or ownership ensure information security programs are funded sufficiently to meet the organization's objectives?
Does the organization have a plan to quickly correct nonconformities, implement actions, and make necessary changes to the information security program?
Is there an approved, documented process for exchanging cybersecurity information with peers, suppliers, or competent authorities on a voluntary basis?
Is there a documented process that requires cybersecurity information-sharing arrangements to be in place before sharing any information, including sensitive information?

Is there a management-approved asset management program that is communicated to constituents and has an owner to maintain, review, and manage IT assets (e.g. systems, hardware, services, and data), and respective controls, throughout their life cycles?
Does the organization implement and manage a Configuration Management Database (CMDB), or asset inventory list, to collect and manage asset-specific information?

Is there an acceptable use policy for information and associated assets that has been approved by management, communicated to appropriate constituents, and assigned an owner to maintain and periodically review the policy?
Are constituents made aware of and held accountable to the Acceptable Use Policy?

Is there a policy or procedure for information handling consistent with its classification that has been approved by management, communicated to appropriate constituents, and assigned an owner to maintain and periodically review (e.g., authorized parties, encryption, public cloud storage, removable media, classification labeling, etc.)?
Does the policy or procedure for information handling include access control requirements including restrictions and authorized parties?
Does the policy or procedure for information handling include encryption requirements?
Does the policy or procedure for information handling include storage requirements including authorized use of Public Cloud storage?
Does the policy or procedure for information handling include electronic transmission security requirements including email, web, and acceptable use by the file transfer services?
Does the policy or procedure for information handling include removable media (e.g., USB Drives, DVDs, etc.) requirements?
Does the policy or procedure for information handling ensure information's classification is properly labeled in its related physical and electronic formats?
Is there a records retention policy and retention schedule covering paper and electronic records, including email in support of applicable regulations, standards, and contractual requirements?
Is there a data retention/destruction requirement that includes information on live media, backup/archived media, and information managed by fourth parties?
Is scoped data included in a regular data retention review?
Is all media containing scoped data disposed of securely?
Is scoped data sent or received electronically?
Are policies, procedures, and controls for transferring information enforced, including the reliability and availability of the transfer service?
Is scoped data sent or received electronically encrypted in transit while outside the network?
Does scoped data sent or received electronically include encryption of e-mail, chat, and other messaging technologies?
Does scoped data sent or received electronically include protection against malicious code by network virus inspection or virus scan at the endpoint?
Does scoped data sent or received electronically include content filtering scans performed on incoming/outgoing email to enforce email policy?
Is regulated or confidential scoped data stored in a database?
Does regulated or confidential scoped data stored in a database include Database Encryption?
Is regulated or confidential scoped data stored in files?
Does regulated or confidential scoped data stored in files include file/folder-level encryption enabled?
Is Information classified according to legal or regulatory requirements, business value, and sensitivity to unauthorized disclosure or modification?
Does the organization assign asset ownership to a department, team or individual?
Are owners responsible to approve and periodically review access to Information Assets?
Is scoped data sent or received via physical media?
Do transport containers protect against physical damage?
Are all the following used when transporting scoped data: chain of custody logs identifying media contents, protection applied, times of transfer and receipt at destination?
Has your company implemented a data loss prevention (DLP) security solution program?
Does the DLP security solution program include a handling process if data loss is suspected or has occurred?
Does the DLP security solution program include a review of the DLP rules at least annually?
Is all organization email scanned by a DLP security solution?
Is all organization outbound web traffic scanned by a DLP security solution?
Is the DLP system configured to block unauthorized traffic?
Do scans performed on incoming and outgoing email include phishing prevention?
Are scoped systems or data stored or transferred in cloud-based public file sharing solutions? If yes, please explain in the Additional Information field.

Are organization-approved, cloud-based public file sharing solution(s) used (e.g., SharePoint, Box, Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive)? If yes, please identify the provider(s) in the Additional Information field.
Are encryption keys managed and maintained for scoped data?
Are encryption keys generated in a manner consistent with key management industry standards?
Are encryption keys encrypted at rest and when transmitted?
Is there segregation of duties between personnel responsible for key management duties and those responsible for normal operational duties?
Is there an option for clients to manage their own encryption keys?
Is Asymmetric encryption key length at a minimum of 2048 bits?
Is maintenance and repair of organizational assets performed and logged, with tools that have been inspected, approved, and controlled?
Can clients specify where their data is stored (logically and physically)?
Is data segmentation and separation capability between clients provided?
Does data segmentation and separation include database segmentation i.e., separate database instances for each client?
In the event of a subpoena or forensics incident, is specific data able to be put on Litigation Hold without impacting other clients' data?
Does your organization have a Data Protection Officer?
Are Human Resources policies and procedures approved by management, communicated to constituents and have an owner to maintain and review?
Are background checks performed pre-employment, and conducted periodically, or at least as frequently as required by industry-specific standards or regulations?
Do Human Resources policies of the organization include constituent background screening criteria (e.g., collection and verification of information) in contractual agreements with third parties?
Are constituents required to sign employment agreements?
Do employment agreements for constituents include acknowledgement of Acceptable Use policies?
Do employment agreements for constituents include acknowledgement of Code of Conduct/Ethics policies?
Do employment agreements for constituents include acknowledgement of Confidentiality/Non-Disclosure policies?

Does senior management require information security personnel to participate in continuing education programs (e.g., online training, webinars, seminars, etc.) to maintain knowledge of changing information security threats and countermeasures?

Does the organization have an implemented human resources security program, including a training and awareness policy that is distributed physically or virtually, (e.g., newsletters, training sessions, website, etc.) with an owner accountable for its oversight?

Are constituents and new hires required to attend security awareness training on an annual basis as part of their terms and conditions of employment or contract, and acknowledge understanding and compliance with the information security policy?
Does the Human Resources security policy include a disciplinary process for non-compliance?
Does the disciplinary process include notification to designated personnel when formal disciplinary actions or sanctions have been initiated?
Does the Human Resources security policy include constituent accountability for the use and misuse of their access credentials?
Does the Human Resources security policy include a process to remove physical and logical access to systems containing scoped data within 24 hours for terminated constituents?
Are exit interviews conducted for terminated constituents that include discussion of concerns regarding security or compliance issues?
Does the organization have an employee performance process that is documented, maintained, and reviewed by management periodically?
Has management approved a physical security program that is communicated to all parties involved, with an assigned owner responsible for maintenance and review?
Does the physical security program include a clean desk policy?
Are there physical security controls for all secured facilities (e.g., data centers, office buildings, physical facility locations)?
Do the physical security controls include electronic-controlled access systems (e.g., key card, token, fob, biometric reader, etc.)?
Does the organization have a policy or procedure for defining security perimeters and the strength of each perimeter in accordance with the information security requirements?
Do the physical security controls include a perimeter physical barrier (e.g., fence or walls)?
Do the physical security controls include entry and exit doors alarmed (forced entry, propped open) and/or monitored by security guards?
Do the physical security controls include a mechanism to prevent tailgating/piggybacking?
Are there physical access authorization controls that include lists of individuals with authorized and restricted access, with logs kept of all access?
Do physical access controls include collection of access equipment (e.g., badges, keys, etc.) and pin numbers changes upon termination or status change?
Do physical access controls include segregation of duties for issuing and approving access?
If visitors are permitted in the facility, are they required to sign in and out and provide a government-issued ID?
Are visitors required to wear a badge distinguishing them from employees, and escorted through secure areas (when applicable)?
Are visitor logs maintained for at least 90 days?
Is there a physical and environmental hazards assessment conducted prior to establishing the location or site of a facility where systems reside?
Has the organization properly identified, monitored, mitigated, and implemented procedures to address physical and environmental threats?
Is signage required to identify environmental controls within the data center?
Do environmental controls include water damage protection measures (e.g., fluid sensors, isolation valves, master shutoff valves)?
Do environmental controls include HVAC and humidity controls?
Do environmental controls include heat and smoke detectors and fire suppression?
Is there a loading dock at the facility?
Is there a battery/UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply) room in offices and/or the facility?
Is there a generator or generator area in offices and/or facility?
Is there a media library to store scoped data?
Is there a telecom equipment room?
Are devices located in a locked server cabinet within the data center?
Do server cabinets include restricted access and are logs kept of all access?
Do the scoped systems and data reside in a data center?
Do other tenants use the data center?
Are locking screensavers on unattended system displays or locks on consoles required within the data center?
Does the organization have maintenance support contracts for critical equipment?
Are tests conducted for any building systems?
Are UPS systems tested at least annually?
Are all security alarm systems tested at least annually?
Are all fire alarms tested at least annually?
Are all fire suppression systems tested at least annually?
Are all generators tested monthly?
Are the organization's Information Technology Operations policies and procedures monitored, aligned with the organizational strategy, and communicated to the entire organization?
Do all IT personnel (e.g., centralized, decentralized, shadow IT) utilize the same set of policies, standards, and procedures?

Does the organization have a formalized Information Technology (IT) structure that defines IT operations (e.g., architecture, infrastructure, service and support, etc.), governance and management responsibilities?

Are IT Governance roles and responsibilities for oversight of the use of outsourced technology or services (e.g., applications, cloud hosting, embedded technology, microservices, etc.) clearly identified and documented?
Are there standard templates utilized by the organization that cover hardware, software, services, and network configurations that are regularly reviewed and updated as necessary?

Is there an operational Change Management/Change Control policy or program that has been documented, approved by management, communicated to appropriate constituents, and assigned an owner to maintain and review the policy?
Does the operational Change Management/Change Control Policy or program include a review for potential operational or security impact (e.g., internal impact, client impact, etc.)?

Does the operational Change Management/Change Control policy or program include categorizing/logging, documenting the changes by severity level (e.g., routine, major, emergency) and securely storing all records?

Does the operational Change Management/Change Control policy or program include a procedure for addressing emergency changes including appropriate approvals after deployment and communication of changes to all relevant constituents?

Does the operational Change Management/Change Control policy or program include changes to the production environment including network, systems, application updates, code and configuration changes subject to the change control process?
Are information security requirements specified and implemented when new systems are introduced, upgraded, or enhanced?
Are new, upgraded, or enhanced systems required to include a determination of security requirements based on the sensitivity of the data?

Are information security specifications for new, upgraded, or enhanced systems identified using requirements from policies and regulations, threat modeling, incident reviews, or use of vulnerability thresholds?
Are business continuity requirements considered for new, upgraded, or enhanced systems?
Does the organization maintain a maintenance policy and procedure for new, upgraded, or enhanced systems?
Is testing for validation of all implemented controls required for new, upgraded, or enhanced systems?
Does the organization have a problem management lifecycle process and ticketing system for tracking, reviewing, and solving issues in a timely manner?
Has management approved an access control policy, communicated it to constituents, appointed an owner to maintain it, and reviewed it?
Are access control procedures reviewed periodically to keep up with changes in business environment, people, processes, and technology?
Are constituents able to access scoped data?
Is there a process for identifying, maintaining, and reviewing access, such as periodic reviews and role changes?
Does the organization maintain and periodically review records of user access rights for information and asset access under the security program?
Are unique IDs required for authentication to applications, operating systems, databases, and network devices?
Are user IDs that identify roles or access levels, or contain personal information other than name or email address (i.e., SSN, Access Level, Admin Role) prohibited?
Is access to applications, operating systems, databases, and network devices provisioned according to the principle of least privilege?
Is there a process to request and receive approval for access to systems prior to transmitting, processing, or storing scoped data?
Is there segregation of duties for granting access and approving access to scoped systems and data?
Are requests for granting access documented, retained and retrievable for audit purposes for a minimum of one year?

Are privileged user access rights restricted to key roles (i.e., constituents with specialized levels of technical access), monitored and reviewed at least quarterly, and documented in the access control policy?
Does the organization have a policy, procedure, or mechanism to ensure that third-parties identities have the required trust level?
Are user access rights reviewed at least annually?
Does the organization's process for assigning or revoking physical or logical access include modifying the access rights of users who have changed roles or jobs?
Does the service provider conduct user and privileged access reviews for remote access by its vendors with access to scoped systems and data?
Are inactive constituent user IDs disabled and deleted after defined periods of inactivity?
Are clients allowed to manage access to their own systems and data?
Is a standards-based federated ID capability available to clients e.g., SAML, OpenID, Single Sign On?
Are clients able to receive a list of personnel that will have access to their scoped system and data?
Is access to systems that store or process scoped data limited by time of day, physical location, or network subnet?
Does the organization have a policy restricting the reuse of user IDs for systems storing or processing scoped data?
Is there an administrative process to address lost, compromised, or damaged authenticators?
Is there a process in the access control system that allows for the revocation of authenticators when necessary?
Have measures been taken to ensure that client-specific data is not accessible without proper authorization through policy controls?
Has management approved, communicated, and enforced a password policy for systems that transmit, process, or store scoped data on all platforms and network devices?
Does the password policy require keeping passwords confidential?
Does the password policy apply to all servers, network devices, web/file transfer services, and workstations?
Does the password policy mandate periodic password changes, prohibit password sharing, and require encryption of passwords in transit and at rest?
Does the password policy require passwords to be encrypted or hashed in storage?
Does the password policy require passwords be masked when entered and displayed by default?
Does the password policy require system configuration to lock an account when five or more invalid login attempts are made?
Does the password policy require password expiration within 90 days or less?
Does the password policy define specific length and complexity requirements for passwords?
Does the password policy define requirements for provisioning and resetting passwords?
Is password reset authority restricted to authorized persons and/or an automated password reset tool?
Are user IDs and passwords communicated/distributed via separate media e.g., e-mail and phone?
Is Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) utilized?
Is Multi-Factor Authentication required for Privileged System Access?
Is Multi-Factor Authentication required for scoped systems and data access?
Is Multi-Factor Authentication available for client accounts?
Is there an internet-accessible self-service portal available that allows clients to provision, audit, modify, and remove user entitlements?
Are documented log-on banner requirements maintained?
Does the organization proactively govern account management of individual, group, system, application, guest, and temporary accounts?
Does the organization have a process for end users to acknowledge and accept remote desktop support prior to another user taking control of their system?
Are identity assertions protected, conveyed, and verified before access to resources is granted?
Are applications used to transmit, process, or store scoped data?
Are the development, testing, and staging environments kept separate from the production environment?
Are development, test, and staging environments separated from the production environment physically and logically?
Is there an individual or group responsible for Application Security?
Do application security experts work with developers for every application?

Is there an established IT Architecture Plan that defines Secure Architectural Design Standards for application security requirements that is approved by management, communicated to constituents, and has an owner to maintain and review?
Are outside development resources utilized?
Is scoped data used in the test, development, or QA environments?
Is authorization required when production data is copied to the test environment?
Is test data destroyed following the testing phase?
Is there formal training for developers that includes security and privacy functions, controls, and mechanisms?
Do changes to applications or application code go through a risk assessment?
Is a security architecture risk analysis performed when new applications are designed?
Are the risks from internal and external sources clearly understood based on risk exposure?
Are system, vendor, or service accounts disabled for normal operations and monitored for usage?
Are web applications configured to follow best practices or security guidelines e.g., OWASP?
Does the organization have a policy or procedure to control access to source code (e.g., designs, specifications) and developmental tools (e.g., compilers, builders, integration tools)?
Do IT support personnel have access to application source libraries?
Is all access to application source libraries logged?
Are audit logs maintained and reviewed for all application source library updates?
Are developers permitted to access production environments, including read only access?
Is application development performed?
Is there a secure software development lifecycle policy that has been approved by management, communicated to appropriate constituents and an owner to maintain, and review the policy?
Is there a formal Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) process?
Is security testing conducted on applications before production?
Do pre-production application security reviews include testing procedures to determine whether security features are effective?
Is there a documented application change management/change control process for applications with scoped data?
Does the application Change Management/Change Control process include change control procedures required for all changes to the production environment?
Does the application Change Management/Change Control process include testing prior to deployment?
Does the application Change Management/Change Control process include management approval prior to deployment?
Does the change management process include stakeholder communication and a list of authorized approvers?
Does the application change management/change control process include an impact assessment to review all affected systems and applications?
Does the application change management/change control process include documentation for all system changes?
Does the application change management/change control process include version control for all software?
Is external code in application documentation identified, reviewed for security flaws, and updated every six months?
Is open-source software or libraries used to transmit, process, or store scoped data?
Are information security reviews conducted and approved for the use or installation of open-source software e.g., Linux, Apache, etc.?
Is a Secure Code Review performed regularly?
Do Secure Code Reviews include regular analysis of vulnerability to recent attacks?
Is a web site or web application supported, hosted, or maintained that processes scoped systems and data?
Are security configuration standards documented for web server software?
Is an Application Programming Interface (API) available to clients?
Is there a formal security program established to include API security reviews and API design?
Is scoped data encrypted in transit within the API for both request and response?
Are APIs tested for security weaknesses?
Can a client manage access to the APIs?
Does the organization have logical or physical segregation between web, application, and database components i.e., Internet, DMZ, Database?

Are web server software security configuration standards reviewed and/or updated at least annually to account for any changes in environment, available security features and/or leading practices and validated against compliant documented software security standards?
Is HTTPS enabled for all web pages?
Are all unnecessary/unused services in web server software uninstalled or disabled?
Are sample applications and scripts removed from web servers?
Are all web server software files maintained separate from the Operating System?
Are available high-risk web server software security patches applied and verified at least monthly?
Are all web server patches, service packs, and hot fixes tested, documented, and approved prior to installation?
Are web server software patch successes and failures logged?
Are third party alert services used to keep up to date with the latest web server software vulnerabilities?
Are web server software versions that no longer have security patches released prohibited?
Are web server software configuration options restricted to authorized users?
Are compilers, editors, or other development tools present in production web server environments?
Are mobile applications that access scoped systems and data developed?
Are any actions performed by the mobile application to access, process, transmit or locally store scoped systems and data?
Has management approved and communicated a Cybersecurity Incident Management Program with a designated owner to maintain and review it?
Has the scope of the Incident Management Program been clearly defined?
Does the organization have a documented Incident Response Plan that outlines the escalation process?
Have the defined stakeholders and organizations received the Incident Response Plan and any updates?

Does the Incident Response Plan include procedures for evidence collection, immediate security incident response, and comprehensive impact assessment, considering service downtime, affected clients or financial counterparts, transaction volume, and potential reputational impact?
Are there any specific notification requirements for customers/clients in the Incident Response Plan's notification procedures?
Is there a specific methodology to regularly review the Incident Response Plan for potential incidents?

Does the Incident Response Plan require identification and mitigation of all vulnerabilities that were exploited, removal of all malware, inappropriate materials, and other components, and remediation of any affected systems discovered after incident closure?
Does the Incident Response Plan include reporting incidents interdepartmentally from logging, help desk, security, and privacy offices?
Is there a specific methodology to regularly review events on scoped systems or systems containing scoped data to uncover potential incidents?
Does regular security monitoring include alerts for malware infections and suspicious activity?
Is there an automated system to review and correlate log and/or behavioral events for Security Information and Event Management (SIEM)?
Is system and data security monitored 24x7x365, including hosted environments?
Does regular security monitoring include daily security alerts for scoped systems and data?
Does regular security monitoring include all changes to user access rights logged on scoped systems and data?
Does regular security monitoring include tracking all changes to privileged user access rights on all systems including those with scoped data?
Does regular security monitoring include Network IDS events?
Does regular security monitoring include behavioral activity indicating botnet traffic?
Does regular security monitoring include network device security events?
Does regular security monitoring include server security events?
Does regular security monitoring include Hypervisor security events?
Does regular security monitoring include application, Web Server, and Database security events?
Is a Cybersecurity Incident/Event Response team available 24x7x365?
Does documentation exist defining which personnel are authorized to speak with the public during adverse events?
Is there a staffed form of communications (e.g., e-mail, web form, phone, etc.) available to customers/clients 24/7/365 to report security incidents?
Is 24x7x365 security event monitoring of the hosting environment performed?
Has the organization outsourced its incident reporting responsibilities to a third-party service provider?
Are incidents promptly categorized and prioritized to respond to those with the most adverse impact on an organization?
Are the actions conducted during an incident investigation formally documented and protected from unauthorized changes?

If the organization is a top-level domain (TLD) registry or domain registrar, is there an established, secure, dedicated database that hosts accurate and complete domain name registration data, including personal data?
Has the organization established a Business Resilience Policy, designated an owner to maintain and review it, and communicated it?
Do the products and/or services specified in the scope of this assessment fall within the scope of the business resilience program?
Does the Business Resilience Program include an individual program owner with full responsibility and accountability?
Have appropriate actions been taken to ensure that constituents working under the Business Resilience Program have acquired the required competencies?
Does the Business Resilience Program include a formal annual (or more frequent) executive management review of business continuity scope, key performance indicators, accomplishments, and risks?

Does the Business Resilience Program's annual review cover resource adequacy (people, tech, support, budget) and report key program activities, short-term/long-term objective attainment, and performance?
Does the annual Business Resilience Program review cover critical suppliers/partners and reflect the current business environment?
Is there documentation available for the Business Resilience Program, and are version and change control processes in place to track the history of changes?
Is there a formal, documented information technology disaster recovery exercise and testing program in place?
Does the disaster recovery exercise and testing program have a designated program owner with full responsibility and accountability?
Does disaster recovery testing include cyber, IT, and privacy resource unavailability, as well as recovery for production data centers, data stores, and critical network infrastructure?
Have measurable recovery objectives been established and are test results evaluated for improvement of all critical systems and resources?
Is there an annual schedule of planned disaster recovery exercises and tests?
Is notification and recovery controls for critical service providers included in the disaster recovery exercise to ensure equivalency with production environment controls?
Are there any dependencies on critical third party service providers?
Are there documented agreements and processes for immediate notification of disruptions from third-party service providers, including critical service providers' contact details?
Are all suppliers of critical hardware, network services and facility services involved in annual continuity and recovery tests?
Have the notification and escalation protocols for key service provider personnel been established?
Have planned responses to business disruptions been coordinated with critical service providers?
When business resilience procedures have been modified that affect key service provider personnel, has a process been implemented to notify them?
Are third parties required to perform, at a minimum, an annual functional disaster recovery test for the service provided?
Are test results and remediation action plans provided by critical service providers after each test?
Are specific availability or service level requirements defined for those third party provided products and/or services?
Is there a pandemic/infectious disease outbreak plan?
Does the pandemic plan include preventive measures, documented strategies, social distancing procedures, testing programs, and oversight programs?
Is scoped data backed up and stored offsite?
Is there a policy or process for the backup of production data?
Are backup integrity and related restoration procedures tested at least annually?
Are backup and replication errors reviewed and resolved as required?
Are offline data backups protected from destructive malware or other threats that may corrupt production and online backup versions of data?
Is there a formal process focused on identifying and addressing risks of disruptive events to business operations (e.g., operational risk assessment?)
Does an Operational risk assessment consider natural, technological, and human-caused hazards and establish risk treatment criteria?
Have formal procedures for business continuity been developed and documented?
Does the organization have a procedure to restore the security of information for critical business processes in the event of an interruption or failure?

Is there a policy or procedure in place within the organization to ensure that there is an appropriate organizational structure ready to deal with any disruptions, with personnel who have the necessary responsibility, authority, and competence to prepare for and mitigate such situations?

Does the organization have a policy or procedure in place to guarantee that its information and communications technology (ICT) continuity plans, which include response and recovery procedures for managing ICT service disruptions, are regularly evaluated through exercises and tests and approved by management?

Do formal business continuity procedures include specific actions to be taken in response to a disruptive event for each business activity/process supporting the products and/or services specified in the scope of this assessment?
Do the formal business continuity procedures include manual steps for critical functions, as applicable?
Do formal business continuity procedures include the continuity of information security activities and processes (e.g., intrusion detection, vulnerability management, log collection)?
Do formal business continuity procedures include the continuity of IT operations activities and processes (e.g., network operations, data center operations, help desk)?
Has senior management assigned the responsibility for overall management of business disruption events, critical response, and recovery efforts to an individual or team?
